rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER WIENS ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER COOKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiffs To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendants Chris Cooke And Haley Anastassiou
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 27, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER WIENS, BRIAN MATHY Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiffs To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendants Chris Cooke And Haley Anastassiou Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses. Hearing Required to address how responses re: materials read could lead to admissible evidence. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT H. MANSBACH VS. LAWRENCE L. MANSBACH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 27, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT LAWRENCE MANSBACH, BETTE MANSBACH MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED. Pursuant to CCP 872.720 the court finds that the Plaintiff is entitled to partition by sale based on the complaint and the answer in this action. The ownership interests of that parties are as alleged in the complaint and admitted in the answer. Hearing Required for Plaintiff to provide authority that a private sale by a broker ordered by the court on a partition action would not require an appointment of a referee. Plaintiff shall provide citations to authorities, with pin cites no later than 9:00 a.m. on August 27, 2020. Supplemental brief not to exceed 1 page. Parties to be prepared to provide the Court with names of (1) two proposed brokers; and (2) two proposed referees, unless the parties can agree on a broker and referee. If parties agree on a broker and referee, parties do not need to provide two names for each category. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JACK W MAHAN VS. JAMES ASHLEY FERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant James Ferry Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 27, 2020 line 5. Defendant James Ferry Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that the reimbursement for expenses and labor that went into the listing and selling of the property is covered by 1983 written partnership agreement and that transfer of $40,000 was a profit sharing by the partners unrelated to a discrete transaction re: portions of front and back yards. = (501/CFH)
